509	\subsection{Inductive-step substrate: per-gap decomposition of
510	  \texorpdfstring{\(\text{pd}(M) = k+1\)}{pd(M) = k+1}}
511	\label{subsec:succ_pd_gap_sequence}
512	
513	The inductive step
514	\cref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}
515	of the Auslander--Buchsbaum formula is gated, as of the current pinned
516	Mathlib snapshot, by exactly three absent substrate ingredients
517	(gaps (1)--(3)) plus a fourth that is now in hand
518	(gap (4), \cref{lem:depth_drops_by_one}). This subsection records the
519	decomposition that the proof block of
520	\cref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd} folds into four bullets:
521	its purpose is to make the dependency graph and the per-gap effort
522	estimates explicit, so that the substrate gaps can be formalised
523	independently (and where the dependency graph permits, in parallel).
524	
525	\paragraph{Sequencing constraint.} The three open gaps are
526	\emph{not} mutually independent at the substrate level. Gap (3)
527	(snake-lemma carving of the minimal resolution by an
528	\(M\)-regular element \(x \in \mathfrak m\)) needs gap (1)
529	(minimal-resolution normalisation: every finite free resolution of
530	a finite module over a Noetherian local ring trims to a minimal one
531	of the same projective dimension) as input, because the snake-lemma
532	output is only known to be a \emph{minimal} resolution of \(M/xM\)
533	over \(R/(x)\) of the same length when the input resolution is itself
534	minimal. Gap (2) (Stacks 00MF, the ``what is exact'' criterion in
535	terms of depths of \(r\)-minors of the transition matrices) is
536	independent of gaps (1) and (3) at the substrate level: it is a
537	self-contained criterion on a complex of finite free modules and
538	feeds the closure assembly without going through gap (1) or gap (3).
539	
540	\paragraph{Dependency diagram.} Read top-to-bottom as ``provides
541	input to'':
542	\begin{verbatim}
543	                gap (1)
544	       minimal-resolution carving
545	   (Stacks lemma-add-trivial-complex)
546	                  |
547	                  v
548	                gap (3)
549	        snake lemma on minimal
550	        resolution under x in m
551	                  |
552	                  v
553	   +--------------+--------------+
554	   |   L1299 closure assembly    |   <--   gap (2)
555	   |   (succ-pd inductive step)  |        Stacks 00MF
556	   +-----------------------------+        "what is exact"
557	                  ^
558	                  |
559	                gap (4)
560	       depth drops by one (CLOSED;
561	       see lem:depth_drops_by_one)
562	\end{verbatim}
563	Gap (4) is already formalised
564	(\cref{lem:depth_drops_by_one}, the project's
565	\texttt{depth\_quotSMulTop\_succ\_eq\_depth\_of\_isSMulRegular})
566	and feeds the closure assembly directly. Gap (2) feeds the closure
567	assembly independently of the gap (1) \(\to\) gap (3) chain.
568	
569	\paragraph{Per-gap effort estimates.} The following table records,
570	for each gap, the estimated formalisation effort (lines of code in
571	the project's Lean source style), a coarse iter budget, the
572	Mathlib-at-\texttt{b80f227} status, and substrate dependencies on
573	other gaps:
574	\begin{center}
575	  \begin{tabular}{|c|l|c|c|c|c|}
576	    \hline
577	    Gap & Statement                              & LOC est.  & Iters
578	        & Mathlib status     & Depends on \\
579	    \hline
580	    (1) & Minimal-resolution carving             & 40--80    & 1--2
581	        & per-syzygy step CLOSED iter-199
582	                                                                & none \\
583	    (2) & Stacks 00MF (``what is exact'')        & 150--200  & 2--3
584	        & absent             & none \\
585	    (3) & Snake lemma on minimal resolution      & ---       & ---
586	        & \textbf{OBVIATED iter-200} & --- \\
587	    (4) & Depth drops by one                     & ---       & ---
588	        & \textbf{CLOSED}    & --- \\
589	    \hline
590	  \end{tabular}
591	\end{center}
592	The gap (1) estimate is anchored to Bruns--Herzog,
593	\emph{Cohen--Macaulay Rings}, Construction~1.5.18 together with the
594	minimal-resolution induction (\S1.5): the trimming algorithm is a
595	finite-rank linear-algebra computation followed by induction on the
596	length of the resolution. The gap (2) estimate reflects the chain
597	Bruns--Herzog Theorem~1.4.13 \(\to\) Eagon--Northcott
598	\(\to\) Buchsbaum--Eisenbud's exactness criterion (Stacks 00MF /
599	\texttt{proposition-what-exact}), which characterises exactness of a
600	complex of finite free modules in terms of depths of the ideals of
601	\(r\)-minors of the transition matrices. Gap (2) is the largest of
602	the three open substrates and is a natural candidate for an upstream
603	Mathlib contribution rather than a project-internal proof.
604	
605	\paragraph{Gap (3) OBVIATED iter-200.} The iter-200 ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB
606	pivot (\ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}) replaces the literal
607	\(\texttt{ChainComplex}\,\mathbb N\) carrier with the abstract
608	\(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) SES-descent pattern; under this
609	pattern, minimality of the resolution is \emph{not} used as input
610	anywhere in the inductive step. Consequently the snake-lemma carving
611	of the minimal resolution (gap (3)) is no longer a substrate
612	requirement: only gap (2) (\(\texttt{pd}\,M > 0 \Rightarrow
613	\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\)) remains the binding gap for
614	the closure of \ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}, and the
615	per-syzygy step of gap (1) is consumed only through the SES at
616	\(0 \to \ker f \to R^n \to M \to 0\) (no minimality input required).
617	See \ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot} for the SES-descent details.
618	
619	\paragraph{Gap (2) Stacks 00MF proof recipe.} The
620	Buchsbaum--Eisenbud exactness criterion (Stacks 00MF /
621	\texttt{proposition-what-exact}) reads: a complex
622	\(\dots \to R^{r_2} \xrightarrow{\varphi_2} R^{r_1}
623	 \xrightarrow{\varphi_1} R^{r_0}\) of finite free modules over a
624	Noetherian local ring \(R\) is exact at \(R^{r_i}\) (for \(i \geq 1\))
625	if and only if \(\texttt{rank}\,\varphi_{i+1} + \texttt{rank}\,\varphi_i
626	= r_i\) and \(\texttt{depth}\,I(\varphi_i) \geq i\), where
627	\(I(\varphi)\) denotes the ideal of \(r\)-minors of \(\varphi\) for
628	\(r = \texttt{rank}\,\varphi\). The recipe for an iter-201+ prover:
629	(a) define \(I(\varphi) \subseteq R\) as the ideal of all
630	\(\texttt{rank}\,\varphi\)-minors of \(\varphi\) (Mathlib supports
631	this via \(\texttt{Matrix.minors}\) and \(\texttt{Ideal.span}\) over the
632	finite index set of \(\binom{n}{r}\) tuples); (b) prove the depth
633	lower bound via induction on \(i\) using a Koszul-complex argument
634	on the regular sequence implicit in the exactness data; (c) derive
635	\(\texttt{pd}\,M > 0 \Rightarrow \texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\)
636	as a corollary by taking the start \(\dots \to R^{r_1} \to R^{r_0} \to
637	M\) of a minimal resolution and applying the criterion at \(i = 1\).
638	Estimated \(\sim 150-200\) LOC project-side; Mathlib upstream PR
639	candidate.
640	
641	\paragraph{Closure assembly (iter-200 ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB pivot).} The
642	iter-200 substrate landing in \ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}
643	replaces the original (1)+(3)-dependent assembly with an SES-descent
644	recipe: given an \(M\) with \(\texttt{pd}_R(M) = k+1\), the
645	per-syzygy step of gap (1)
646	(\ref{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}) supplies a
647	surjection \(f : R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\); the SES
648	\(0 \to \ker f \to R^n \to M \to 0\) plus the iter-200 helpers
649	\(\texttt{hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ker\_of\_surjection}\) +
650	\(\texttt{hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_succ\_of\_hasProjectiveDimensionLT\_ker}\)
651	give \(\texttt{pd}_R(\ker f) = k\) exactly; gap (2) (the
652	Buchsbaum--Eisenbud criterion at the start of a minimal resolution)
653	delivers \(\texttt{depth}\,M < \texttt{depth}\,R\); the iter-200
654	helper
655	\(\texttt{depth\_ker\_ge\_min\_of\_surjection\_finite\_localRing}\)
656	gives \(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f \geq \min(\texttt{depth}\,R,
657	\texttt{depth}\,M + 1) = \texttt{depth}\,M + 1\); a complementary LES
658	upper bound (or the IH applied to \(\ker f\) under the strict
659	descent of \(\texttt{pd}\)) gives the matching upper bound. Combine
660	to get \(\texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,M + 1\), then apply
661	the IH (\(\texttt{pd}\,\ker f = k\)) for
662	\(k + \texttt{depth}\,\ker f = \texttt{depth}\,R\), which rearranges
663	to \((k+1) + \texttt{depth}\,M = \texttt{depth}\,R\) as required.
664	Estimated closure-assembly cost (gaps in hand): \(\sim 50-80\) LOC
665	inductive code.
666	
667	\paragraph{Iter budget refinement.} Aggregating the per-gap
668	estimates with the closure assembly: gap (1) at 1--2 iters, gap (2)
669	at 2--3 iters, gap (3) at 1--2 iters, and the closure assembly
670	at 1 iter gives a total remaining iter budget of \textbf{5--8 iters}
671	for \cref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd}. The schedule may
672	parallelise across the gap (2) branch and the gap (1) \(\to\) gap (3)
673	chain --- so wall-clock could be as low as
674	\(\max(\text{gap (2)},\, \text{gap (1)} + \text{gap (3)}) +
675	\text{closure}\), i.e.\ \(\max(2\text{--}3,\, 2\text{--}4) + 1\),
676	yielding 3--5 iters in the parallel-best case. The substrate
677	sequencing constraint (gap (3) requires gap (1)) is hard; the gap
678	(2) branch is genuinely independent of that chain and could be
679	dispatched as a parallel prover lane the moment a free slot opens.
680	This estimate refines the earlier ``~6--12 iters'' coarse budget
681	recorded in \texttt{STRATEGY.md}: the refined window is somewhat
682	tighter on the upper end (5--8 vs.\ 6--12 sequential) and is
683	substantially tighter under parallel dispatch (3--5 parallel-best
684	vs.\ 6--12).

686	\subsection{Gap (1) first-step substrate (iter-199): per-syzygy
687	  minimal surjection from a finite free module}
688	\label{subsec:ab_gap1_first_step}
689	
690	\begin{lemma}
691	
692	\leanok
693	  [Per-syzygy minimal surjection from \(R^n\) for finite modules
694	    over a local ring]
695	  \label{lem:exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}
696	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing}
697	  \uses{def:depth}
698	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 00LK
699	  % (lemma-add-trivial-complex; minimal-resolution carving substrate)
700	  % (read from references/stacks-algebra.tex, L24590-L24623).
701	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
702	  % "Suppose $R$ is a ring. Let
703	  %     \ldots
704	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i + 1}}
705	  %     R^{n_i}
706	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_i}
707	  %     R^{n_{i-1}}
708	  %     \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i-1}}
709	  %     \ldots
710	  %  be a complex of finite free $R$-modules. Suppose that for some $i$
711	  %  some matrix coefficient of the map $\varphi_i$ is invertible.
712	  %  Then the displayed complex is isomorphic to the direct sum of a
713	  %  complex
714	  %     \ldots \to
715	  %     R^{n_{i + 2}} \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i + 2}}
716	  %     R^{n_{i + 1}} \to
717	  %     R^{n_i - 1} \to
718	  %     R^{n_{i - 1} - 1} \to
719	  %     R^{n_{i - 2}} \xrightarrow{\varphi_{i - 2}}
720	  %     R^{n_{i - 3}} \to
721	  %     \ldots
722	  %  and the complex $\ldots \to 0 \to R \to R \to 0 \to \ldots$ where
723	  %  the map $R \to R$ is the identity map."
724	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 00LK
725	    (\texttt{lemma-add-trivial-complex}).}
726	  Let \(R\) be a local ring (Noetherian-ness NOT required at this
727	  step), \(\mathfrak m \subseteq R\) the maximal ideal,
728	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\) the residue field, and \(M\) a finite
729	  \(R\)-module. Then there exist \(n \in \mathbb N\) and an
730	  \(R\)-linear surjection \(f : R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\) such that:
731	  \begin{enumerate}
732	    \item \(n = \dim_\kappa(\kappa \otimes_R M)\) (the minimal
733	      generator count of \(M\), via Nakayama);
734	    \item \(\ker f \subseteq \mathfrak m \cdot R^n\) (the
735	      ``minimality'' condition: every entry of every relation lives
736	      in the maximal ideal, so the chosen lift has no trivial
737	      summands).
738	  \end{enumerate}
739	\end{lemma}
740	\begin{proof}
741	  Pick a \(\kappa\)-basis
742	  \(b : \mathrm{Fin}\,n \to \kappa \otimes_R M\) via
743	  \texttt{Module.finBasis} with
744	  \(n = \dim_\kappa(\kappa \otimes_R M)\). Surjectivity of the
745	  canonical map \(M \twoheadrightarrow \kappa \otimes_R M\)
746	  (composition of \texttt{TensorProduct.mk\_surjective} and
747	  \texttt{Ideal.Quotient.mk\_surjective}) gives lifts
748	  \(m_i \in M\) with \(1 \otimes m_i = b_i\). Define
749	  \(f : R^n \to M\) by \(f x := \sum_i x_i \cdot m_i\) via
750	  \texttt{(Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m}.
751	
752	  Surjectivity: \(\mathrm{range}\, f = \mathrm{span}_R(m_i)\) by
753	  \texttt{Module.Basis.constr\_range}, and
754	  \(\mathrm{span}_R(m_i) = M\) by Nakayama via
755	  \texttt{IsLocalRing.span\_eq\_top\_of\_tmul\_eq\_basis}.
756	
757	  Kernel containment: for \(x \in \ker f\) the relation
758	  \(\sum_i x_i m_i = 0\) tensors to
759	  \(\sum_i (1 \otimes x_i \cdot m_i) = 0\); using
760	  \texttt{TensorProduct.tmul\_smul} and the linear independence of
761	  \(b\) (\texttt{Module.Basis.linearIndependent}) we obtain
762	  \(\mathrm{residue}_R(x_i) = 0\) for every \(i\), i.e.\
763	  \(x_i \in \mathfrak m\). Decomposing
764	  \(x = \sum_i x_i \cdot \mathrm{Pi.single}\,i\,1\) then yields
765	  \(x \in \mathfrak m \cdot R^n\) by
766	  \texttt{Submodule.smul\_mem\_smul} and
767	  \texttt{Submodule.sum\_mem}.
768	\end{proof}
769	
770	This lemma is the \emph{per-syzygy} step of gap (1)'s
771	minimal-resolution carving: given a finite \(R\)-module \(M\)
772	over a local ring \(R\), it produces a minimal surjection
773	\(R^n \twoheadrightarrow M\). Iter-200's ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB pivot
774	(\ref{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}) replaces the original
775	\(\mathbb N\)-recursive \(\texttt{ChainComplex}\) assembly target
776	with abstract \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) SES descent: only
777	the per-syzygy SES is consumed; the iterated chain-complex carrier
778	is not needed.
779	
780	\subsection{Iter-200 ALIGN\_WITH\_MATHLIB pivot: \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) SES descent}
781	\label{subsec:ab_gap1_haspdlt_pivot}
782	
783	The original iter-199 plan for closing
784	\ref{lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd} carried the
785	\(\mathbb N\)-recursive minimal free resolution as a literal
786	\(\texttt{ChainComplex}\,\mathbb N\,(\texttt{ModuleCat}\,R)\) object,
787	proved finite-rank-free at each level, and then attacked the
788	inductive step via the snake lemma (gap (3)). The
789	\texttt{ab-natrecursive} mathlib-analogist verdict (iter-200,
790	persistent file \texttt{analogies/ab-natrecursive.md}) rejected this
791	route as 3--4\(\times\) over budget and gated on a Mathlib-absent
792	termination-of-syzygy-tower lemma. The recommended alignment is to
793	descend pd via Mathlib's
794	\(\texttt{CategoryTheory.HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) predicate, which
795	takes \(\texttt{ShortComplex.ShortExact}\) input and an extremal
796	projectivity hypothesis on \(X_1\) or \(X_3\) to give
797	\(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\) on the other piece. No
798	\(\mathbb N\)-recursive carrier and no minimality content are
799	required.
800	
801	\begin{lemma}
802	\leanok
803	[Bridge: \(\texttt{projectiveDimension}\,M = n \Rightarrow
804	  \texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\,M\,(n+1)\)]
805	  \label{lem:hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq}
806	  \lean{RingTheory.Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq}
807	  \uses{def:projective_dimension}
808	  \textit{Source: Mathlib's \(\texttt{CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension\_lt\_iff}\).}
809	  For a Noetherian local ring \(R\) and a finite \(R\)-module \(M\)
810	  with \(\texttt{Module.projectiveDimension}\,R\,M =
811	  ((n : \mathbb N) : \texttt{WithBot}\,\mathbb N_\infty)\), one has
812	  \(\texttt{HasProjectiveDimensionLT}\,(\texttt{ModuleCat.of}\,R\,M)\,
813	  (n+1)\). Single-rewrite proof via Mathlib's

1198	lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
1199	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1200	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] :
1201	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
1202	      Function.Surjective f ∧
1203	      n = _root_.Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1204	        (TensorProduct R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) M) ∧
1205	      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
1206	  set κ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField R with hκ
1207	  set n := _root_.Module.finrank κ (TensorProduct R κ M) with hn
1208	  -- Pick a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M`.
1209	  let b : _root_.Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (TensorProduct R κ M) :=
1210	    _root_.Module.finBasis κ (TensorProduct R κ M)
1211	  -- The canonical map `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` is surjective.
1212	  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
1213	    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
1214	    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1215	  -- Lift each basis element to a representative in M.
1216	  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
1217	  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
1218	  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
1219	  -- Define `f` by sending each standard basis vector of `Fin n → R` to `m i`.
1220	  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
1221	  -- Evaluation: `f x = Σ x i • m i`.
1222	  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
1223	    intro x
1224	    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
1225	        _root_.Module.Basis.constr_apply]
1226	    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
1227	      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
1228	    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
1229	    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
1230	  -- Range = span of `m`.
1231	  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
1232	    _root_.Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
1233	  -- Nakayama: span of `m i` equals all of `M`.
1234	  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
1235	    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
1236	  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
1237	  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
1238	  · -- Kernel containment in `𝔪 • ⊤`.
1239	    intro x hx
1240	    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
1241	    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
1242	    -- Apply `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` to `Σ x i • m i = 0`.
1243	    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : TensorProduct R κ M) := by
1244	      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
1245	    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1
1246	    -- Rewrite each summand: `1 ⊗_R (x i • m i) = residue(x i) • b i`.
1247	    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
1248	        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
1249	      intro i
1250	      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1251	          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
1252	        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
1253	      rw [hm i]; rfl
1254	    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1255	        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
1256	      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
1257	    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
1258	    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
1259	    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
1260	      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
1261	        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
1262	      exact fun i => this i

1400	For an R-linear map `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` between standard free modules over a
1401	commutative ring R, A decomposes as `A = ∑_{(i,j)} A_{i,j} • E_{i,j}` where
1402	`A_{i,j} = (A (Pi.single j 1)) i` is the matrix entry and `E_{i,j}` is the
1403	"elementary" linear map sending `Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1`. From this
1404	decomposition combined with R-bilinearity of `Ext.comp` and the axiom-clean
1405	helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator`, we obtain the matrix-collapse
1406	result: if every entry of A lies in `Ann_R N`, then the induced postcomposition
1407	`Ext^p(N, R^m) → Ext^p(N, R^n)` via `mk₀ (ofHom A)` is the zero map.
1408	
1409	This is the key new substrate for closing the **Path B base case `pd M = 1`**
1410	of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula: given a minimal surjection `f : R^n ↠ M`
1411	with `ker f` free of positive rank, the inclusion `ker f ≅ R^k ↪ R^n` is an
1412	R-linear map with entries in 𝔪 (by minimality `ker f ≤ 𝔪 • ⊤`); the
1413	matrix-collapse then forces the LES injectivity needed to conclude
1414	`depth M < depth R`. -/

1417	`Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1` and all other standard basis vectors to 0. -/
1418	private def elemMap {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ) (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) :
1419	    (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
1420	  (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) ∘ₗ
1421	    (LinearMap.proj (R := R) (φ := fun _ : Fin m => R) j)

1423	/-- The elementary map `E_{i,j}` evaluated at `x : R^m` gives `Pi.single i (x j)`. -/
1424	private lemma elemMap_apply {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (n m : ℕ)
1425	    (i : Fin n) (j : Fin m) (x : Fin m → R) :
1426	    (elemMap n m i j : (Fin m → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R)) x = Pi.single i (x j) := by
1427	  classical
1428	  change (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R)
1429	    (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R)) (x j) = Pi.single i (x j)
1430	  rw [LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply]
1431	  ext k
1432	  rw [Pi.smul_apply]
1433	  by_cases hk : k = i
1434	  · subst hk; rw [Pi.single_eq_same, Pi.single_eq_same, smul_eq_mul, mul_one]
1435	  · rw [Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, Pi.single_eq_of_ne hk, smul_zero]

1521	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1522	
1523	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1524	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1525	```
1526	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1527	```
1528	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1529	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1530	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1531	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1532	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1533	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1534	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1535	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1536	
1537	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/

1782	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings

## Module.elemMap (line ~1418)
- **Approach:** Defined `elemMap n m i j : R^m →ₗ R^n` as
  `(toSpanSingleton R (Fin n → R) (Pi.single i 1)) ∘ₗ (LinearMap.proj j)`.
  This is the "elementary matrix" linear map sending `Pi.single j 1 ↦ Pi.single i 1`
  and other basis vectors to 0.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## Module.elemMap_apply (line ~1425)
- **Approach:** Direct calculation via `LinearMap.toSpanSingleton_apply` +
  `Pi.single_eq_same` / `Pi.single_eq_of_ne` case-split.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## Module.linearMap_finFunR_matrix_decomp (line ~1442)
- **Approach:** Show A = ∑_{(i,j)} A(Pi.single j 1) i • elemMap n m i j by
  (a) decomposing the input `x = ∑_j (x j) • Pi.single j 1`,
  (b) pushing through `A` via `map_sum + map_smul`,
  (c) expressing each row `A(Pi.single j 1) = ∑_i (A(Pi.single j 1) i) • Pi.single i 1`,
  (d) reshuffling the double sum via `Finset.sum_product_right`,
  (e) closing via `LinearMap.smul_apply + elemMap_apply`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## Module.ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator (line ~1481)
- **Approach:** The matrix-collapse on Ext per the iter-201 analogist
  `ab-stacks00mf` Path B recipe. Given `A : R^m →ₗ R^n` with all entries
  `A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ Ann_R N`, the postcomposition
  `e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom A)) (add_zero p)` is zero in `Ext^p(N, R^n)`. Proof
  chain: matrix decomposition `A = ∑ A_ij • E_ij`, then push through
  `ofHom`, `mk₀`, and `Ext.comp` using
  `ModuleCat.hom_sum + mk₀_sum + comp_sum` and
  `ModuleCat.hom_smul + Ext.mk₀_smul + Ext.comp_smul`. Each summand reduces
  to `A_ij • (e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom (elemMap _ _ i j))))`, where the scalar
  `A_ij ∈ Ann_R N` makes the summand zero via the existing
  `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` helper (line 229).
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean.

## auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd (line 1517, body NOT CLOSED)
- **Approach attempted (Path B from analogist):** With the matrix-collapse
  helper now in hand, the closure plan is:

  **Base case `k = 0` (pd M = 1)** — ~50-80 LOC residual:
  1. From `_hpd : projectiveDimension R M = 1`, derive
     `HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ker f) 1` for a minimal surjection
     `f : R^n →ₗ M` (using iter-200 helpers
     `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq` +
     `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection`).
  2. `K := ker f` is finite projective over local Noetherian R, hence free:
     `Module.Flat.of_projective` + `Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing`.
     Pick a basis to get an iso `φ : (Fin k → R) ≃ₗ[R] K`.
  3. Compose `A := K.subtype ∘ₗ φ.toLinearMap : (Fin k → R) →ₗ R^n`.
     Its image equals `K = LinearMap.ker f ⊆ 𝔪 • ⊤` (from minimality
     clause `_hf_min` of `exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing`).
  4. Show every entry `A (Pi.single j 1) i ∈ 𝔪 = Ann_R κ`:
     - Use `ideal_smul_top_pi_const` (line 863) to identify
       `𝔪 • ⊤_{Fin n → R}` with the pointwise condition `∀ i, x i ∈ 𝔪`.
     - Apply at the column `A (Pi.single j 1) ∈ K ⊆ 𝔪 • ⊤`.
     - `Ideal.annihilator_quotient` then identifies `𝔪 = Ann_R κ`.
  5. Apply `ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator` (NEW)
     with `A` to get `e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom A)) = 0` for all
     `e : Ext^p(κ, R^k)`.
  6. Transport to the subtype: `subtype = A ∘ₗ φ.symm.toLinearMap`, so via
     `ofHom_comp` and `Ext.comp` associativity, the postcomposition map
     `Ext^p(κ, K) → Ext^p(κ, R^n)` (via the SES first morphism) is also zero.
  7. LES of `Ext^*(κ, -)` on the SES `0 → K → R^n → M → 0`:
     `covariant_sequence_exact₂` at `i = depth R`: the map
     `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, M) →^δ Ext^{depth R}(κ, K)` has image equal to
     `ker(Ext^{depth R}(κ, K) → Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^n)) = Ext^{depth R}(κ, K)`
     (since the next map is zero). Together with `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, R^n) = 0`
     (depth `R^n = depth R > depth R - 1`), we get `δ` is an iso. Since
     `Ext^{depth R}(κ, K) = Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^k) ≠ 0` (K is free of rank k ≥ 1,
     depth K = depth R, so this Ext index doesn't vanish), we conclude
     `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, M) ≠ 0`, i.e., `depth M ≤ depth R - 1`,
     i.e., `depth M + 1 ≤ depth R`.
  8. The matching `depth M + 1 ≥ depth R` from `depth_of_short_exact`
     part (2): `min(depth R^n, depth K - 1) ≤ depth M`, i.e.,
     `min(depth R, depth R - 1) = depth R - 1 ≤ depth M` (when depth R ≥ 1).
  9. Combine for `depth M + 1 = depth R`. Edge case `depth R = 0`:
     the LES at `i = 0` gives `Ext^0(κ, K) = 0` (LHS is 0, RHS is 0 by matrix-collapse),
     but `Ext^0(κ, K) ≠ 0` since `depth K = 0`. Contradiction shows we cannot
     have `pd M = 1` and `depth R = 0` simultaneously.
  10. Also need to rule out `k = 0` (i.e., K = 0): if K = 0, then `f` is iso, so
      M is projective, so `pd M = 0`, contradicting `pd M = 1`.

  **Inductive step `k + 1, k ≥ 1`** — ~30-50 LOC residual:
  1. Same setup: minimal surjection f, get `HasProjectiveDimensionLT K (k+1)`.
  2. From `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker`
     (contrapositive): `pd K = k` exactly.
  3. Apply main theorem `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` at `n = k` on K (NOT
     via `_succ_pd` k itself, but via the main theorem dispatching to
     `_succ_pd (k-1)` when k ≥ 1). [Needs `_succ_pd` to be structured as
     induction on k, generalizing M, for the IH to be available.]
  4. IH gives `k + depth K = depth R`, hence `depth K = depth R - k`.
  5. From step 4 + LES at `i = depth K` (using matrix-collapse on
     A : R^? → R^n where R^? is the iso of K = free of rank `R^?_rank`...
     wait — in this case K has pd k ≥ 1, K is NOT free. So matrix-collapse
     doesn't apply directly).
  6. **The inductive-step problem:** for k ≥ 1, K = ker f has pd k ≥ 1, so K
     is NOT projective. Matrix-collapse needs K to be free. So the inductive
     step needs a different argument.
  
  **Inductive step alternative (LES + IH only):**
  - The SES `0 → K → R^n → M → 0` plus IH `k + depth K = depth R`.
  - `depth_of_short_exact (2)`: `min(depth R^n, depth K - 1) ≤ depth M`,
    i.e., `min(depth R, depth R - k - 1) = depth R - k - 1 ≤ depth M`
    (when `depth R ≥ k + 1`).
    So `depth M ≥ depth R - k - 1`, i.e., `depth M + k + 1 ≥ depth R`.
  - For the matching ≤: use `depth_of_short_exact (3)`:
    `min(depth R^n, depth M + 1) ≤ depth K = depth R - k`. So either
    `depth R ≤ depth R - k` (impossible for k ≥ 1) or
    `depth M + 1 ≤ depth R - k`, i.e., `depth M + k + 1 ≤ depth R`.
  - Combined: `depth M + k + 1 = depth R`, i.e., `(k+1) + depth M = depth R`.

  **So the inductive step k ≥ 1 closes via IH + `depth_of_short_exact`
  parts (2) and (3) alone — NO matrix-collapse needed.** The matrix-collapse
  is only used for the base case k = 0.

- **Result:** NOT ADDED (residual `sorry` at L1574 unchanged).
- **Why not landed this iter:** The base case requires ~50-80 LOC of LES

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): ℕ∞ combine for the
inductive step of Auslander–Buchsbaum.

Pure arithmetic in `ℕ∞ = WithTop ℕ`: packages the inductive hypothesis
`j + depth(K) = depth(R)` together with the two `depth_of_short_exact`
inequalities (parts (2) and (3)) on the SES `0 → K → R^n → M → 0` — after
identifying `depth(R^n) = depth(R)` — into the conclusion
`(j+1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`. Valid for `j ≥ 1` (the inductive step
`pd M ≥ 2`); the `j = 0` / `pd M = 1` base case is handled separately via
matrix-collapse since part (3) is then vacuous. -/
private lemma enat_ab_inductive_combine {j : ℕ} {d dK dM : ℕ∞}
    (hIH : (j : ℕ∞) + dK = d)
    (h2 : min d (dK - 1) ≤ dM)
    (h3 : min d (dM + 1) ≤ dK)
    (hj : 1 ≤ j) :
    ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) + dM = d := by
  subst hIH
  cases dK with
  | top =>
    -- `dK = ⊤`: part (2) forces `dM = ⊤`, both sides are `⊤`.
    have hdM : dM = ⊤ := by
      have : (⊤ : ℕ∞) ≤ dM := by simpa using h2
      simpa using top_le_iff.mp this
    subst hdM; simp
  | coe K =>
    cases dM with
    | top =>
      -- `dM = ⊤`: part (3) gives `↑(j+K) ≤ ↑K`, i.e. `j ≤ 0`, contradiction.
      exfalso
      have : (j : ℕ∞) + (K : ℕ∞) ≤ (K : ℕ∞) := by simpa using h3
      rw [← Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_le] at this
      omega
    | coe m =>
      -- All finite: reduce parts (2),(3) to `ℕ` facts and finish with `omega`.
      have e2 : K - 1 ≤ m := by
        have h2' : min ((j : ℕ∞) + (K : ℕ∞)) ((K : ℕ∞) - 1) ≤ (m : ℕ∞) := h2
        rw [show ((K : ℕ∞) - 1) = ((K - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) by
              cases K with
              | zero => simp
              | succ K => push_cast; rfl,
            ← Nat.cast_add, ← Nat.cast_min, Nat.cast_le] at h2'
        omega
      have e3 : min (j + K) (m + 1) ≤ K := by
        have h3' : min ((j : ℕ∞) + (K : ℕ∞)) ((m : ℕ∞) + 1) ≤ (K : ℕ∞) := h3
        rw [show ((m : ℕ∞) + 1) = ((m + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) by push_cast; rfl,
            ← Nat.cast_add, ← Nat.cast_min, Nat.cast_le] at h3'
        exact h3'
      rw [← Nat.cast_add, ← Nat.cast_add, Nat.cast_inj]
      omega

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): exact projective dimension
of the syzygy `ker f`.

For a surjection `f : R^n ↠ M` with `pd_R M = k+2`, the kernel satisfies
`pd_R (ker f) = k+1` exactly. The upper bound `≤ k+1` is the syzygy-descent
helper `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection` packaged through
`projectiveDimension_le_iff`; the lower bound `≥ k+1` is the contrapositive of
the ascent helper `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker`
through `projectiveDimension_ge_iff` (if `pd(ker f) < k+1` then `pd M < k+2`,
contradiction). This is the exact-pd input the inductive step needs to invoke
its induction hypothesis on `ker f`. -/
private lemma projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
    {n : ℕ} (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
    {k : ℕ}
    (hpdM : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M = ((k + 2 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)) :
    _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f) = ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
  have hM3 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) (k + 2 + 1) :=
    Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq hpdM
  have hK2 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) (k + 2) :=
    Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection f hf (k := k + 1) hM3
  have hle : _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
      ≤ ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
    rw [show _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
          = CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) from rfl,
        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_le_iff]
    exact hK2
  have hge : ((k + 1 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞)
      ≤ _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f) := by
    rw [show _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R (LinearMap.ker f)
          = CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) from rfl,
        CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_ge_iff]
    intro hK1
    have hM2 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) (k + 2) :=
      Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker f hf (k := k) hK1
    have hlt : CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)
        < ((k + 2 : ℕ) : WithBot ℕ∞) :=
      CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension_lt_iff.mpr hM2
    rw [show CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)
          = _root_.Module.projectiveDimension R M from rfl, hpdM] at hlt
    exact absurd hlt (lt_irrefl _)
  exact le_antisymm hle hge

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): both `depth_of_short_exact`
inequalities (parts (2) and (3)) for the SES `0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0`.

Packages parts (2) and (3) of Stacks 00LE applied to the kernel SES of a
surjection `f : R^n ↠ M` from a finite free module of rank `n ≥ 1` over a
Noetherian local ring, after identifying `depth(R^n) = depth(R)` via
`depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`. These are precisely the two inequalities
fed to `enat_ab_inductive_combine` to close the inductive step of the
Auslander–Buchsbaum formula. -/
lemma depth_ses_ineqs_of_surjection_finite_localRing
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] [Nontrivial M]
    {n : ℕ} (hn : 1 ≤ n) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M) (hf : Function.Surjective f)
    [Nontrivial (LinearMap.ker f)] :
    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) - 1)
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R)
          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1)
      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (LinearMap.ker f) := by
  haveI : Inhabited (Fin n) := ⟨⟨0, hn⟩⟩
  haveI : Nonempty (Fin n) := ⟨default⟩
  haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n → R) := Pi.nontrivial
  haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _
  have hex : Function.Exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f :=
    LinearMap.exact_subtype_ker_map f
  have hinj : Function.Injective (LinearMap.ker f).subtype :=
    Subtype.val_injective
  have htriple := depth_of_short_exact (LinearMap.ker f).subtype f hinj hf hex
  have heq : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin n → R)
      = depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R :=
    depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty _
  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
  · have h2 := htriple.2.1
    rwa [heq] at h2
  · have h3 := htriple.2.2
    rwa [heq] at h3

/-! ### Helper iter-202 Lane AB Path B (axiom-clean): nonzero `Ext` at the depth
index.

The converse read-off of `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`: for a nonzero finite
module `M` of depth exactly `↑D` over a Noetherian local ring, there is a
nonzero element of `Ext^D_R(κ, M)`. (Below `↑D` all `Ext` vanish; the strict
failure of vanishing at `↑(D+1)` must therefore occur at index `D`.) This is
the input that exhibits a nonzero class in the base case of Auslander–Buchsbaum:
`Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^k) ≠ 0`. -/
lemma exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
    [Nontrivial M] {D : ℕ}
    (hD : depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M = (D : ℕ∞)) :
    ∃ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
        (ModuleCat.of R M) D, e ≠ 0 := by
  -- Below `D`, all Ext vanish.
  have hvanish : ∀ i : ℕ, i < D → ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
      (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 :=
    (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) D).mp (by rw [hD])
  -- `↑(D+1) ≤ depth M` fails, so vanishing below `D+1` fails.
  have hnle : ¬ ((D + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
    rw [hD, Nat.cast_le]; omega
  rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (D + 1)] at hnle
  push_neg at hnle
  obtain ⟨i, hi, e, he⟩ := hnle
  have hiD : i = D := by
    by_contra hne
    have : i < D := by omega
    exact he (hvanish i this e)
  subst hiD
  exact ⟨e, he⟩

      Module.exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing R M

    have hM_lt : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) 2 :=
      Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_projectiveDimension_eq _hpd
    haveI hK_lt : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) 1 :=
      Module.hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker_of_surjection f hf_surj (k := 0) hM_lt
    haveI hKproj : CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R (LinearMap.ker f)) :=
      inferInstance
    haveI : _root_.Module.Projective R (LinearMap.ker f) :=
      (IsProjective.iff_projective _).mpr hKproj
    haveI : _root_.Module.Flat R (LinearMap.ker f) := _root_.Module.Flat.of_projective
    haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R (LinearMap.ker f) := Module.IsNoetherian.finite R _

      _root_.Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing

      rw [← not_subsingleton_iff_nontrivial]
      intro hsub

        rw [eq_bot_iff]
        intro x hx
        rw [Submodule.mem_bot]

      let e : (Fin n → R) ≃ₗ[R] M := LinearEquiv.ofBijective f ⟨hfinj, hf_surj⟩

      haveI hMproj : CategoryTheory.Projective (ModuleCat.of R M) :=
        (IsProjective.iff_projective M).mp inferInstance
      have hMlt1 : HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ModuleCat.of R M) 1 := inferInstance

      · exact h

    have hk : 1 ≤ k := by
      rw [hk_def]

    haveI : Nontrivial (Fin n → R) := Pi.nontrivial
    haveI : Nontrivial (Fin k → R) := Pi.nontrivial
    let φ : (Fin k → R) ≃ₗ[R] LinearMap.ker f :=
      (_root_.Module.finBasis R (LinearMap.ker f)).equivFun.symm
    let A : (Fin k → R) →ₗ[R] (Fin n → R) :=
      (LinearMap.ker f).subtype ∘ₗ (φ : (Fin k → R) →ₗ[R] LinearMap.ker f)
    have hAinj : Function.Injective A :=

    have hdRk : Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) (Fin k → R)

    · -- Direction (B): `1 + depth M ≤ depth R` via the matrix-collapse LES.

      · rw [htop]; exact le_top

        rcases D with _ | D'

            intro hcontra

      calc Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R
          ≤ Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R - 1 + 1 := le_tsub_add
        _ ≤ Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M + 1 := add_le_add_right hpart2 1
        _ = 1 + Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := add_comm _ _

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean

## Session summary (iter-202, Lane AB-Path-B-Close)

**HARD BAR MET.** `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` body is **fully
closed axiom-clean** (no `sorry`), and the 3 requested `private` removals
landed. The main theorem `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` is now
axiom-clean (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`). This closes the
16+-iter Lane AB gap.

- **Sorry count (file): 1 → 0.**
- Built axiom-clean: 4 new helper declarations + closed `_succ_pd` body.
- 3 `private` removals (Step 3) done.

## Declarations added (4, all axiom-clean)

1. `RingTheory.enat_ab_inductive_combine` (private lemma, ~line 1551) —
   pure `ℕ∞` arithmetic: from IH `j + depth K = depth R`, `depth_of_short_exact`
   parts (2),(3), and `j ≥ 1`, derives `(j+1) + depth M = depth R`. Casework
   on `WithTop ℕ` + `min_le_iff` + `omega`. **RESOLVED.**
2. `RingTheory.projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection` (private lemma,
   ~line 1611) — for `f : R^n ↠ M` with `pd M = k+2`, `pd (ker f) = k+1`
   exactly. Squeeze via `projectiveDimension_le_iff` (upper) +
   `projectiveDimension_ge_iff` (lower, contrapositive of the ascent helper).
   **RESOLVED.**
3. `RingTheory.Module.depth_ses_ineqs_of_surjection_finite_localRing`
   (lemma, ~line 1411) — both `depth_of_short_exact` parts (2),(3) on the
   kernel SES `0 → ker f → R^n → M → 0`, with `depth(R^n)=depth(R)`
   identified via `depth_pi_const_eq_depth_of_nonempty`. **RESOLVED.**
4. `RingTheory.Module.exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq` (lemma, ~line 1450) —
   converse read-off of `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`: depth `= ↑D` (finite)
   ⟹ `∃ nonzero e : Ext^D(κ, M)`. **RESOLVED.**

## auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd (now ~line 1801) — RESOLVED, axiom-clean

- **Approach:** Restructured as `induction k generalizing M` per the
  iter-201 handoff recipe.
  - **Inductive step `pd M = k+2`** (no matrix-collapse): minimal surjection
    `f : R^n ↠ M`; `projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection` gives
    `pd (ker f) = k+1` exactly; `Nontrivial (ker f)` from `pd ≠ ⊥`; IH on
    `ker f`; `depth_ses_ineqs_…`; combine via `enat_ab_inductive_combine`.
  - **Base case `pd M = 1`** (Path B matrix-collapse): `ker f` is projective
    (pd < 1) → free (`free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing`); pick `φ : R^k ≃ₗ ker f`
    (`k ≥ 1` via `φ` + subsingleton argument); build SES
    `0 → R^k →[A] R^n →[f] M → 0` where `A`'s entries lie in `𝔪 = Ann κ`
    (minimality `ker f ≤ 𝔪•⊤` + `ideal_smul_top_pi_const`). Direction (A)
    `depth R ≤ 1 + depth M` from `depth_of_short_exact (2)`. Direction (B)
    `1 + depth M ≤ depth R`: case `depth R = ⊤` trivial; case `↑D` uses
    `exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq` to get nonzero `α : Ext^D(κ, R^k)`,
    matrix-collapse (`ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator`)
    kills `α.comp (mk₀ (ofHom A))`; sub-case `D=0` contradiction via
    `postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono`; sub-case `D=D'+1` uses
    `covariant_sequence_exact₁` to transport `α` to a nonzero
    `Ext^{D'}(κ, M)`, contradicting `↑D ≤ depth M`, hence
    `depth M + 1 ≤ depth R` via `Order.add_one_le_of_lt`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — `#print axioms` on the main theorem = kernel triple.

## Step 3 — `private` removals (DONE)

Promoted to public (all axiom-clean, proofs unchanged):
- `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`
- `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.isDomain_of_regularLocal`  ← **NOTE namespace**
- `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`
  ← **NOTE namespace**

**Important for Lane COE (iter-203):** the PROGRESS.md plan referred to these
as `RingTheory.isDomain_of_regularLocal` and
`RingTheory.regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq`, but they live
in the **`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay`** namespace. The cross-file imports must
use the fully-qualified names above. (They were not moved/renamed — only the
`private` keyword was dropped — so the namespace is preserved.)

## Summary

- **Declarations added (4):** `enat_ab_inductive_combine`,
  `projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection`,
  `Module.depth_ses_ineqs_of_surjection_finite_localRing`,
  `Module.exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq`.
- **Declarations closed (1):** `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` body
  (was `sorry`; now complete, axiom-clean).
- **Private removals (3):** as listed in Step 3.
- **Sorry count (file): 1 → 0.**
- **Axiom verification:** `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula`,
  `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`, all 4 new helpers, and both promoted
  CohenMacaulay helpers each return `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## Why I stopped

**Real progress / objective complete.** The Lane AB HARD BAR is met:
`_succ_pd` body closed axiom-clean (4 new helper lemmas + induction
restructuring), and the 3 `private` removals landed. The main
Auslander–Buchsbaum theorem is now fully axiom-clean. The iter-202
`progress-critic route202` user-escalation pre-commitment is **satisfied
and discharged** (Lane AB did NOT return PARTIAL).

PUSH-BEYOND (main `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` inductive step) was already
discharged: the main theorem delegates its `n>0` branch to `_succ_pd`, which
is now fully closed, so the main theorem is complete with no further work.

## Blueprint markers (for review agent / sync_leanok)

The following should now resolve to `\leanok` (proofs closed, axiom-clean):
- `lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`
  (`RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`)
- `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (`RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula`)

The blueprint NOTE on `lem:auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` requesting
option (1) (remove `private`) is now **fully resolved** — the `private` was
removed and the body closed. The review agent may drop that NOTE block.

The gap-table in `\subsec:succ_pd_gap_sequence` can be updated: **all gaps

## Summary

I closed the **16+-iteration Lane AB gap**: the Auslander–Buchsbaum inductive-step lemma `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` is now **fully proved, axiom-clean**, and the main theorem `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` verifies with only the kernel triple `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

**HARD BAR met** (body axiom-clean + 3 `private` removals). Verified via `lake env lean` (exit 0).

### What landed (file sorry count: 1 → 0)

**4 new axiom-clean helper lemmas:**
1. `enat_ab_inductive_combine` — pure `ℕ∞` arithmetic combining the IH with the two `depth_of_short_exact` inequalities.
2. `projectiveDimension_ker_eq_of_surjection` — exact `pd(ker f) = k+1` via the `projectiveDimension_le_iff`/`_ge_iff` squeeze.
3. `Module.depth_ses_ineqs_of_surjection_finite_localRing` — both SES depth inequalities (parts 2 & 3).
4. `Module.exists_ne_zero_ext_of_depth_eq` — nonzero `Ext^D(κ,M)` at the depth index (converse of `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`).

**`_succ_pd` restructured** as `induction k generalizing M`:
- **Inductive step** (`pd ≥ 2`): syzygy descent + IH + combine, no matrix-collapse.
- **Base case** (`pd = 1`): the Path B matrix-collapse — free kernel `R^k ↪ R^n` with entries in `𝔪`, `ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_…` kills `Ext^D(κ,R^k)→Ext^D(κ,R^n)`, and `covariant_sequence_exact₁` transports a nonzero class to `Ext^{D-1}(κ,M)`. Depth-finiteness was sidestepped by a `depth R = ⊤` / `↑D` case split. **Gap (2) Stacks 00MF was obviated** — no "what is exact" criterion needed.

**3 `private` removals** (Step 3) — note the namespace correction flagged for Lane COE iter-203: `isDomain_of_regularLocal` and `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` live under **`RingTheory.CohenMacaulay`**, not `RingTheory` as PROGRESS.md assumed.

The iter-202 `progress-critic` user-escalation pre-commitment is **satisfied and discharged** — Lane AB did not return PARTIAL.
